reasoning: The document contains multiple major structural and fidelity issues. These include displaced hyperlinks that were extracted as standalone elements, a footnote incorrectly inserted into the main text flow, and several missing hyperlinks (including a bare URL). With exactly four major defects, the document falls squarely into the HUMAN_REVIEW category based on count thresholds.

concerns.analysis: At the bottom of page 4, the source PDF has a footnote ('1 Levee Terms & Definitions (army.mil)'). The main text continues on page 5 ('only one application needs to be...'). In the JSON output, the footnote text is incorrectly inserted directly into the main reading flow, appearing before the text from page 5 in the same paragraph block.
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
1 Levee Terms & Definitions (army.mil)

only one application needs to be submitted by the COG.
concerns.output_quote: 1 Levee Terms & Definitions (army.mil) only one application needs to be submitted by the COG.

concerns.description: Link text pulled over but not hyperlinked
concerns.analysis: Several hyperlinks in the source PDF were flattened to plain text in the JSON output without corresponding anchor tags or link objects. For example, 'training video' on page 1, 'Levee Terms & Definitions (army.mil)' on page 4, and 'Division's website.' on page 5 are all clickable links in the PDF but appear as unlinked text in the output.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: training video (page 1) / Levee Terms & Definitions (army.mil) (page 4) / Division's website. (page 5)

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 3 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=3, examples=['https://deq.nc.gov/about/divisions/water-infrastructure/i-ne', 'https://deq.nc.gov/about/divisions/water-infrastructure/i-ne', 'https://www.epa.gov/G3/learn-about-green-streets']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: 1 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: (army.mil
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=1. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links. Per NCDIT criteria, link text not wrapped in href is a major defect.
Several portions of text are explicitly underlined in the source document for emphasis, but this emphasis is lost in the extracted JSON output.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: ...constructing or repairing dams or other structures designed to retain waters within a stream channel...
concerns.output_quote: ...constructing or repairing dams or other structures designed to retain waters within a stream channel...

concerns.description:
Header/footer elements retained in output (1): LASII Stormwater Program Questions and Answers
LASII Program
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Source PDF contains 2 instance(s) of blue-styled text without hyperlink annotations. These appear to be intended as hyperlinks but were not annotated in the PDF (likely lost during Word-to-PDF export). This is a source document limitation, not a pipeline defect. Examples: page 4: "Levee Terms & Definitions (army.mil)"; page 5: "Division's website."
concerns.analysis: orphaned_blue_text_count=2. Blue text without link annotations detected via PyMuPDF color extraction.

action_items: Move the footnote text to its correct position or mark it up appropriately.
Fix displaced standalone URLs to ensure they appear inline within their respective paragraphs.
Manually restore broken or missing hyperlinks, including the bare URL.
Remove retained header, footer, and page number artifacts.
